body: The moment Seraphina Grant saw her fiancé's hand on her stepsister's pregnant belly, she canceled the wedding, burned the dress, and signed a contract marriage with the "dying" heir everyone in New York pitied. What no one satisfying knew—the man they called a "corpse on a ventilator" had a resting heart rate of 72 and a net worth of $40 billion. And Seraphina? The "talentless sister" they mocked? She was Dr. S. Grant—the anonymous surgeon who had just been offered the International Hartwell Prize for pioneering a cardiac procedure that saved three hundred lives last year. But she didn't correct them. Not yet. Standing in the penthouse bathroom of the Kensington Hotel, Seraphina peeled off her engagement ring and dropped it into the toilet. The five-carat diamond made a satisfying *plunk*
